channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Author claims Trump's popularity declines amid economic challenges and ineffective leadership
item.description: Author Kurt Andersen, former founder of Spy Magazine, argues that President Donald Trump's image as an entertaining alternative in U.S. politics is diminishing due to ongoing economic pressures. Speak...

item.title: Sydney and Melbourne property prices ease as higher rates and policy changes cool housing market
item.description: House prices in Australia’s largest cities have weakened, with Sydney and Melbourne recording monthly declines amid ongoing affordability pressures, higher interest rates, and recent policy changes af...

item.title: Parliamentary disclosures highlight ministerial calendar error, Albanese gifts, and increased Taiwan travel
item.description: This Crikey column from the ‘The Horse’s Mouth’ series examines a mix of political disclosures and behind-the-scenes developments involving Australian federal politics and international engagement. It...

item.title: Federal audit launched into Indigenous Languages Commissioner’s office following anonymous complaints and governance concerns
item.description: The federal government has launched an independent financial audit into the Office of the Commissioner of Indigenous Languages after receiving anonymous complaints regarding its operations and governa...

item.title: Political controversy over depiction of Thiruvalluvar in saffron attire in Tamil Nadu
item.description: A political controversy has broken out in Tamil Nadu over the depiction of revered Tamil poet and philosopher Thiruvalluvar in saffron-coloured attire during a government-linked event. The issue emerg...

item.title: Hamilton's Chinese Community Thrives Without an Official Chinatown
item.description: Hamilton, Ontario, never developed an official Chinatown, yet its Chinese community has persisted and grown through grassroots efforts and community spaces. Despite early proposals in the 1980s to cre...

item.title: BJP finalises RS and MLC candidates ahead of nominations
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) held a meeting of its Central Election Committee at Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence to decide the party’s candidates for the Rajya Sabha elections and state L...

item.title: Rajasthan Government Takes Strict Action Against Corrupt and Negligent Officials
item.description: The Rajasthan government has launched a stringent anti-corruption drive, resulting in the dismissal of 20 officials and suspension of 332 others. Chief Minister Bhajanlal Sharma has emphasised that pu...

item.title: Russell Henley wins Charles Schwab Classic in playoff after late surge at Colonial
item.description: Russell Henley captured the Charles Schwab Classic at Colonial in dramatic fashion, defeating Eric Cole in a playoff after a strong late comeback. Henley birdied the final three holes of regulation to...

item.title: Madagascar Rural Bridge Improves Vanilla Transport to International Markets
item.description: A small but important bridge in northeastern Madagascar has become a key link in the global vanilla supply chain, helping farmers move their harvests from remote farming areas to international markets...

item.title: Exorcism practices in the Philippines increase amid concerns over mental health misdiagnosis
item.description: The article explores the growing use of exorcisms in the Philippines and the concerns raised by mental health professionals about potential misdiagnosis of serious psychiatric conditions. Mary Rose, a...

item.title: Former addicts share how early cocaine use led to long-term addiction and life disruption
item.description: The article presents the personal stories of four individuals who developed serious cocaine addictions after first experimenting with the drug during their teenage years or early adulthood. Each case ...

item.title: Dutch authorities allow Kanye West concerts despite controversial statements
item.description: US rapper Kanye West, also known as 'Ye', has been granted permission to perform two concerts in Arnhem, Netherlands, in June, despite widespread controversy over his antisemitic comments and previous...

item.title: Why Melbourne’s preference for black clothing reflects its climate and cultural identity
item.description: The article explores why Melbourne has developed a strong cultural association with wearing black clothing, arguing that the trend is shaped by a combination of environmental, historical, and cultural...

item.title: Verulam shooting linked to ongoing gang turf war following death of Anthony Gounden
item.description: Violence has escalated in Verulam following the assassination of alleged gang figure Anthony 'Tatu' Gounden, with authorities and private security reporting further deadly incidents linked to an ongoi...

item.title: Kingston childcare centre destroyed in early morning fire, police investigate
item.description: A major fire destroyed the Treehouse Early Learning Centre on Juers Street, Kingston, south of Brisbane, in the early hours of Monday morning. At least six fire crews were called to the scene around 1...

item.title: Centre launches Khet Bachao Abhiyan to brace for weak monsoon and promote balanced input use
item.description: The Indian government is preparing for a challenging sowing season due to forecasts of a weak monsoon and rising costs of inputs like fertilizers and diesel. To mitigate potential agricultural losses,...

item.title: Experts Investigate Suspected Ebola Cases in Brazil Amid Central African Outbreak
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in central Africa has claimed around 250 lives and is spreading more rapidly than aid organisations can respond. The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has reported over 1,000 c...

item.title: Severe winter weather warning issued for South Africa with snow, heavy rain and rough seas expected
item.description: The South African Weather Service (SAWS) has issued warnings for a significant winter weather system expected to affect large parts of the country during the first week of June. Conditions are forecas...

item.title: Sandiganbayan to Review Plunder Case Against Senator Jinggoy Estrada
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada’s legal situation is currently in the hands of the Sandiganbayan Fifth Division, which will decide whether to give due course to his motions seeking judicial determination of p...
